title: Design Specification
transcript_text:
DOCUMENT ID: SCL-555-DS-001
DATE: 2026-01-20
DEPARTMENT: Design & TCAD
LOT ID: LOT-2026-01

DESIGN RULE MANUAL AND DEVICE SPECIFICATION REPORT: SCL-555
This specifications document outlines the layout parameters and baseline design rules for the SCL-555 High-Speed Automotive Controller. The fabrication of this device will be executed using the Semiconductor Complex Limited (SCL) standard CMOS technology process.

1. TECHNOLOGY PROFILE
- Node Reference: SCL-CMOS-0.25G (Double-metal, single-poly baseline).
- Substrate Type: P-type (100) silicon, 10-15 Ohm-cm resistivity.
- Well Configuration: Twin-tub process with Retrograde N-well.
- Core Voltage: 2.5V nominal supply voltage.

2. METAL 1 INTERCONNECT RULESET
- Minimum Metal 1 Width (M1_W_min): 0.25 um (micro-meters).
- Minimum Metal 1 Spacing (M1_S_min): 0.25 um.
- Metal 1 Thickness: 5500 Angstroms (Al-Cu alloy).
- Sheet Resistance Target (Rs_M1): 0.08 +/- 0.01 Ohm/sq.

3. METAL 2 INTERCONNECT RULESET
- Minimum Metal 2 Width (M2_W_min): 0.35 um.
- Minimum Metal 2 Spacing (M2_S_min): 0.35 um.
- Metal 2 Thickness: 9000 Angstroms.
- Sheet Resistance Target (Rs_M2): 0.05 +/- 0.01 Ohm/sq.

4. PARAMETRIC YIELD TARGETS
- Target Wafer Sort Yield (WSY): Minimum 90.0% acceptable baseline.
- Parametric CD Deviation: Max +/- 0.02 um across all critical lithography levels.
- Gate Oxide Thickness (Tox): 55 Angstroms +/- 3 Angstroms.

CRITICAL DISPOSITION RULE:
Any deviations from the specified Metal 1 line width or spacing configurations must be formally approved by the TCAD modeling department (supervised by Dr. A. K. Sharma). Such changes require comprehensive photolithography and etch process simulation using Synopsys TCAD before mask tooling tape-out.

title: Design Review Meeting
transcript_text:
DOCUMENT ID: SCL-555-MM-001
DATE: 2026-02-10
DEPARTMENT: Design & TCAD
LOT ID: LOT-2026-01

TECHNICAL MINUTES OF MEETING: SCL-555 BASELINE LAYOUT HANDOFF & DRC REVIEW
Location: Cleanroom Conference Room 3
Attendees: Dr. A. K. Sharma (Design & TCAD), Mr. R. K. Verma (Fabrication Operations), Ms. S. Iyer (Quality Assurance)

AGENDA:
Review and approval of the Design Rule Checking (DRC) and Layout Versus Schematic (LVS) reports for the SCL-555 High-Speed Automotive Controller baseline layout prior to mask writing and silicon handoff for Lot 1.

DISCUSSION TOPICS & ANALYSIS:

1. Photolithography Process Window
Dr. Sharma presented the Focus Exposure Matrix (FEM) simulation data for the 0.25 um Metal 1 spacing layout. The simulation was conducted utilizing the i-line 365nm stepper with a numerical aperture (NA) of 0.60. The results demonstrate a comfortable process window with a depth of focus (DOF) of 0.15 um at a nominal exposure dose of 220 mJ/cm2. The photoresist profile has an average sidewall angle of 88.5 degrees, which is sufficient for anisotropic plasma etching.

2. Dry-Etch Chamber Uniformity and Chemistry
Mr. Verma reported on the baseline dry-etch process parameters. He noted that the Dry-Etch-04 chamber (Applied Materials Centura platform) was recently calibrated. A series of test runs utilizing dummy oxide wafers coated with Al-Cu films showed cross-wafer etch rate uniformity within 3.2%. The dry-etch recipe CL_ETCH_M1_0.25 will be utilized as the baseline process for the Metal 1 layer. This recipe employs a chlorine-based chemistry with a flow rate ratio of 80 sccm Cl2 and 40 sccm BCl3, coupled with an RF source power of 800W and a bias power of 150W to ensure clean trench profiles.

3. Reliability and Quality Checkpoints
Ms. Iyer reviewed the quality checklist. Because the SCL-555 is slated for automotive safety certifications, she requested that Lot 1 undergo full reliability screening, including 1000 hours of High-Temperature Operational Life (HTOL) and thermal stress cycling. She confirmed that the dicing and wire bonding parameters are compliant with packaging standards.

DECISIONS & DISPOSITION:
1. The SCL-555 baseline layout design database is approved for handoff.
2. Lot 1 (Lot ID: LOT-2026-01) is cleared for mask writing and immediate fabrication start.
3. The dry-etch process for Metal 1 is locked to recipe CL_ETCH_M1_0.25 on the Dry-Etch-04 chamber.

title: Shrink Approval Meeting
transcript_text:
DOCUMENT ID: SCL-555-MM-002
DATE: 2026-05-15
DEPARTMENT: Design & TCAD
LOT ID: LOT-2026-02

MINUTES OF MEETING: SCL-555 LAYOUT SHRINK AND DENSITY OPTIMIZATION REVIEW
Location: Cleanroom Conference Room 3
Attendees: Dr. A. K. Sharma (Design & TCAD), Mr. R. K. Verma (Fabrication Operations), Ms. S. Iyer (Quality Assurance)

AGENDA:
Evaluation of proposed layout density modifications to satisfy customer demands for lower component packaging footprints and higher die counts.

DISCUSSION TOPICS & ANALYSIS:

1. Layout Footprint Reduction
Dr. Sharma presented a plan to implement a linear layout shrink to a 0.18 um minimum Metal 1 spacing rule for the next run (Lot 2). By scaling down the layout dimensions, the Gross Die Per Wafer (GDPW) will increase from 450 to 620, representing an approximate 37% increase in density. Dr. Sharma emphasized that the shrink would allow SCL to fulfill the volume requirements within the tight delivery schedule demanded by the automotive client.

2. Dry-Etch Process Capability Concerns
Mr. Verma raised process safety concerns regarding the 0.18 um layout shrink. He explained that shrinking the spacing from 0.25 um to 0.18 um increases the aspect ratio of the metal trenches. Under these conditions, the legacy dry-etch recipe CL_ETCH_M1_0.25 on the Dry-Etch-04 chamber might experience severe micro-loading (etch rate retardation in narrow gaps), leading to incomplete metal clearance. He recommended a 4-week recipe development cycle to adjust the chlorine-to-boron-trichloride ratio and optimize the over-etch step.

Dr. Sharma responded that the customer timeline cannot accommodate a 4-week process development delay. He argued that the Dry-Etch-04 chamber has high-density plasma capability, which should theoretically handle the 0.18 um spacing without modifying the recipe, as the aspect ratio remains under 3:1.

3. Reliability Risks
Ms. Iyer cautioned that proceeding with the layout shrink without recipe qualification could introduce high-density bridging defects, which would compromise the Wafer Sort Yield and final package reliability. However, due to severe commercial schedule pressure, she agreed that if the engineering team proceeded immediately, Quality Assurance would monitor the Wafer Sort Yield very closely.

DECISIONS & DISPOSITION:
1. Approved layout shrink to 0.18 um minimum Metal 1 line spacing for Lot 2.
2. Lot 2 (Lot ID: LOT-2026-02) is cleared for immediate fab release to meet customer delivery dates.
3. The fabrication run will proceed using the existing dry-etch tool set. No new recipe qualification cycle will be performed prior to Lot 2 execution.

title: Lot 1 Fab Run Report
transcript_text:
DOCUMENT ID: SCL-555-FAB-001
DATE: 2026-03-05
DEPARTMENT: Fabrication Operations
LOT ID: LOT-2026-01

WAFER FABRICATION INLINE METROLOGY AND RUN SUMMARY - LOT 1
This report details the inline manufacturing measurements and parametric results for the SCL-555 Lot 1 wafers.

1. PROCESS ROUTING DETAILS
- Product Code: SCL-555-MCU-V1
- Lot ID: LOT-2026-01 (Total: 25 wafers)
- Substrate Batch: SCL-P100-W20
- Lithography Tool: Stepper-02 (i-line, 365nm light source)
- Dry Etch Tool: Dry-Etch-04 chamber

2. INLINE METROLOGY SUMMARY
- Photoresist Thickness (After Develop Inspection - ADI): 1.25 um.
- Metal 1 ADI Critical Dimension (M1 Spacing): 0.252 um average.
- Metal 1 After Etch Inspection (AEI) Critical Dimension (M1 Spacing):
  - Target: 0.250 um.
  - Measured Mean: 0.248 um.
  - Standard Deviation (3-sigma): 0.012 um.
  - Etch Bias: -0.004 um (well within standard limits).
- Oxide Isolation Step Height: 3500 Angstroms.

3. PARTICLE AND DEFECT DENSITY LOGS
- Post-Metal 1 Etch Defect Density (KLA-Tencor inspection):
  - Average Particle Count: 12 per wafer (Specification: < 30 per wafer).
  - Scratch/Residue Excursions: 0.
  - Resist Peeling/Delamination: 0.

4. ELECTRICAL WAFER SORT (EWS) PARAMETRIC OUTCOMES
- Wafer Sort Yield (WSY):
  - Lot Average: 92.4%.
  - Peak Wafer Yield: 94.1% (Wafer #08).
  - Minimum Wafer Yield: 90.8% (Wafer #19).
- Contact Resistance (Rc_M1_to_Silicon): 1.8 Ohms/contact.
- Sheet Resistance (Rs_M1): 0.081 Ohm/sq.
- Gate Oxide Leakage (Ig_leak): < 1.0 pA/um2.

DISPOSITION:
Lot 1 inline measurements and final EWS yields are stable and fully compliant with the 0.25 um design specification rules. Wafers are cleared for back-end packaging and assembly.

title: Lot 1 Packaging Report
transcript_text:
DOCUMENT ID: SCL-555-PKG-001
DATE: 2026-03-12
DEPARTMENT: Packaging & Assembly
LOT ID: LOT-2026-01

BACK-END ASSEMBLY PACKAGING & RELIABILITY INSPECTION REPORT: LOT 1
This report details the back-end assembly operations and mechanical integrity inspections completed for SCL-555 Lot 1 wafers.

1. PACKAGING FLOW DETAILS
- Wafer Batch: SCL-555-MCU-V1, Lot 1 (25 wafers)
- Package Type: 64-pin LQFP (Low-profile Quad Flat Package, copper leadframe).
- Die Attach Epoxy: Ablebond 84-1LMISR4 conductive silver epoxy.
- Wire Type: 1.0 mil (25 um) diameter Gold (Au) wire.
- Molding Compound: Sumitomo G700 Epoxy Molding Compound (EMC).

2. ASSEMBLY PROCESS MEASUREMENTS
- Wafer Dicing: Diced utilizing dicing saw Disco DFD6340 with diamond blades. Dicing yield was 99.85%.
- Die Attach Inspection:
  - Curing parameters: Cured at 150C for 60 minutes in nitrogen oven.
  - Void Analysis (Scanning Acoustic Microscopy - CSAM): Average void area measured at 4.2% of die area. Specification is < 10%.
- Wire Bonding: Completed on K&S IConn high-speed wire bonder. Gold wire ball bonding parameters:
  - Average wire pull strength: 8.4 grams (Requirement: > 3.0g).
  - Average ball shear force: 12.5 grams (Requirement: > 8.0g).
  - Bond pull failure mode: 100% mid-span wire breaks, zero interface lifts.
  - Test sample size: 100 units from 5 different wafers.

3. MOLDING & SEALING
- Transfer Molding: Performed at 175C mold temperature, 90 seconds clamp time. Post-mold cure (PMC) executed at 175C for 4 hours.
- Visual Inspection: 0% voiding or lead frame deformation observed.
- Package Hermeticity (Fine/Gross Leak Testing): Passed standard MIL-STD-883 leak testing.

DISPOSITION:
Lot 1 packaging assembly has met all mechanical and interface specifications. The wire-bond shear force averages 12.5g and wire pull strength averages 8.4g, showing strong joint stability. Wafers are approved to proceed to Quality Qualification.

title: Lot 1 Quality Clearance
transcript_text:
DOCUMENT ID: SCL-555-QA-001
DATE: 2026-03-20
DEPARTMENT: Quality Assurance
LOT ID: LOT-2026-01

QUALITY REVIEW AND COMMERCIAL RELIABILITY CLEARANCE REPORT: LOT 1
This report details the final reliability stresses and qualification results performed on the SCL-555 Lot 1 packages.

1. QUALIFICATION WORKFLOW AND STRESS LEVELS
To clear the SCL-555 MCU for automotive-grade applications, Lot 1 sample packages were subjected to three primary environmental stresses in accordance with AEC-Q100 standards.

2. ENVIRONMENTAL STRESS TESTS
- High-Temperature Operational Life (HTOL):
  - Sample Size: 100 units.
  - Stress Parameters: 1000 hours at 125C with dynamic clock signals and 2.75V supply voltage (10% over-voltage stress).
  - Parametric checks: Completed at 168h, 500h, and 1000h.
  - Result: 0 failures, 0 parametric drift shifts observed.
- Temperature Cycling (TC):
  - Sample Size: 100 units.
  - Stress Parameters: 500 cycles from -40C to 125C, air-to-air chamber, 15-minute dwell time.
  - Diagnostics: CSAM scan at 500 cycles.
  - Result: 0 delamination failures, 0 package cracking.
- Highly Accelerated Stress Test (HAST):
  - Sample Size: 50 units.
  - Stress Parameters: 96 hours at 130C and 85% relative humidity under 2.5V static bias.
  - Result: 0 leakage excursions, 0 corrosion events.

3. ELECTRICAL METROLOGY AUDIT
Post-stress electrical measurements show:
- Standby leakage current (Idd_standby): Mean of 1.4 uA (Limit: < 10 uA).
- Output High Voltage (Voh): Mean of 2.42V at 4mA load (Limit: > 2.2V).
- Threshold Voltage (Vth) parametric shift: < 15 mV.

DISPOSITION:
Lot 1 (LOT-2026-01) has successfully passed all AEC-Q100 equivalent reliability qualification stress tests. No gate oxide breakdown, parameter shift, or packaging integrity loss occurred. The lot is formally cleared for commercial release and shipment to the customer.

title: Yield Excursion Review
transcript_text:
DOCUMENT ID: SCL-555-MM-003
DATE: 2026-06-01
DEPARTMENT: Quality Assurance
LOT ID: LOT-2026-02

YIELD EXCURSION INVESTIGATION REPORT: LOT 2 (LOT-2026-02)
Location: Failure Analysis Lab, QA Department
Chair: Ms. S. Iyer (Quality Assurance)
Attendees: Dr. A. K. Sharma (Design & TCAD), Mr. R. K. Verma (Fabrication Operations)

BACKGROUND:
Upon completion of the wafer fabrication process routing for SCL-555 Lot 2 (LOT-2026-02), the Electrical Wafer Sort (EWS) returned a final yield average of 45.8%. This represents a critical yield excursion event, falling significantly below the 90.0% minimum quality threshold. An urgent failure analysis and root cause investigation was initiated.

FAILURE ANALYSIS & METROLOGY FINDINGS:

1. Scanning Electron Microscopy (SEM) and Focused Ion Beam (FIB) Diagnostics
The Failure Analysis (FA) team selected 5 failing dies from different wafers (Wafer #03, #07, #12, #18, #22) representing center and edge layout locations.
- Under SEM examination, extensive aluminum-copper metal bridging shorts were detected between adjacent lines on the Metal 1 layer.
- FIB cross-sections revealed that the trench profiles between Metal 1 interconnect lines had a vertical thickness of 5500 Angstroms, but the bottoms of these trenches were not fully cleared. A residual metal layer ranging from 400 to 1200 Angstroms thick remained in the spacing gaps, directly shorting adjacent signals.
- Spacing measurements between the Metal 1 lines at ADI (After Develop Inspection) were verified at 0.18 um +/- 0.01 um, confirming that the lithography stepper printed the shrunk layout dimensions correctly.

2. Fabrication Process Log Audit
The investigation team performed a detailed audit of the manufacturing logs for LOT-2026-02 in the Fabrication Operations database.
- The logs show that when LOT-2026-02 reached the Metal 1 dry-etch step, the operators ran the lot using the standard baseline recipe CL_ETCH_M1_0.25 on the Dry-Etch-04 chamber.
- This recipe (CL_ETCH_M1_0.25) employs a total etch time of 120 seconds, which was developed and optimized for the 0.25 um spacing rule layout (Lot 1).
- Because the layout spacing of Lot 2 was shrunk to 0.18 um, the aspect ratio of the gaps increased. This reduction in width restricts the diffusion of chlorine reactive radicals ($Cl*$) into the narrow trenches and impedes the out-diffusion of volatile etch by-products ($AlCl_3$). This phenomenon is known as RIE lag or micro-loading.
- As a direct consequence of the micro-loading effect, the etch rate inside the 0.18 um spaces was reduced by approximately 22% compared to the open fields. Running the legacy recipe CL_ETCH_M1_0.25 resulted in under-etching of these narrow spaces, leaving behind the residual metal bridging observed in the SEM and FIB analysis.

CORRECTIVE ACTIONS & DISPOSITION:
1. The 45.8% Wafer Sort Yield is confirmed to be caused by Metal 1 bridging shorts due to dry-etch under-clearance under RIE lag.
2. The legacy dry-etch recipe CL_ETCH_M1_0.25 must not be used for layouts with features under 0.25 um.
3. For all future 0.18 um spacing layout runs, the etching recipe must be updated to CL_ETCH_M1_0.18 on the Dry-Etch-04 chamber. This new recipe extends the etch time by 20% (to 144 seconds) and adjusts the Cl2/BCl3 ratio to enhance the vertical chemical etching component.
4. Lot 2 (LOT-2026-02) is rejected for delivery and quarantined in the QA hold locker.

title: Lot 2 Fab Run Report

FABRICATION RUN REPORT: LOT 2 (LOT-2026-02)

title: Lot 2 Packaging Report
transcript_text:
DOCUMENT ID: SCL-555-PKG-002
DATE: 2026-06-05
DEPARTMENT: Packaging & Assembly
LOT ID: LOT-2026-02

PACKAGING REPORT: LOT 2 (LOT-2026-02)

ASSEMBLY LOT STATUS:
Lot 2 was placed on hold pending QA disposition. Partial packaging completed on 200 units.

PROCESS PERFORMANCE:
1. Die Bonding: 200 units mounted on BGA substrates
2. Wire Bonding: Gold wire (25um) on all signal pads
3. Wire Pull Strength: Mean 8.5g (spec >5.0g)
4. Mold Compound: Epoxy resin, transfer molded at 175°C

ISSUES IDENTIFIED:
- Die attach voiding observed in 12 units (6% rate)
- Voids concentrated at die corners (>25% void area in 4 units)
- Likely cause: outgassing from epoxy die-attach paste during cure

DISPOSITION:
Packaging lot quarantined pending QA decision on Lot 2 overall yield.

title: Lot 2 Quality Disposition
transcript_text:
DOCUMENT ID: SCL-555-QA-002
DATE: 2026-06-08
DEPARTMENT: Quality Assurance
LOT ID: LOT-2026-02

LOT 2 QUALITY DISPOSITION: LOT-2026-02

QUALITY ASSESSMENT:
1. Wafer Sort Yield: 45.8% (FAIL - threshold 90.0%)
2. Package Assembly: 6% die attach voiding rate
3. Reliability HTOL: Not initiated (lot on hold)

FAILURE CLASSIFICATION:
- Critical: Metal 1 bridging shorts due to RIE lag
- Major: Die attach voiding in corner regions
- Minor: Parametric Vth drift on edge dies

DISPOSITION DECISION:
Lot 2 (LOT-2026-02) is REJECTED per SCL-QA-REJ-2026-001.
All 450 wafers placed in QA quarantine locker.
Customer notification of delivery delay to be issued.

title: Post-Excursion Redesign
transcript_text:
DOCUMENT ID: SCL-555-RD-001
DATE: 2026-06-10
DEPARTMENT: Design & TCAD
LOT ID: LOT-2026-02

POST-EXCURSION REDESIGN REVIEW: LOT 2 (LOT-2026-02)

Attendees: Dr. A. K. Sharma, Mr. R. K. Verma, Ms. S. Iyer

AGENDA:
Root cause review of Lot 2 yield excursion and layout revision planning.

DISCUSSION:
1. Yield excursion confirmed at 45.8% due to M1 bridging shorts
2. RIE lag identified as primary mechanism on Dry-Etch-04 with legacy CL_ETCH_M1_0.25 recipe
3. TCAD simulations show that for 0.18um spacing with 5500A M1 thickness, the aspect ratio of 3.05:1 requires 25% longer etch time

DECISIONS:
1. Metal 1 width revised back to 0.22um for Lot 3, spacing to 0.25um
2. New etch recipe CL_ETCH_M1_0.18 qualified for future 0.18um-node designs
3. Process Change Notice (PCN-2026-001) issued for dry-etch toolset

title: Dry Etch Recipe Update
transcript_text:
DOCUMENT ID: SCL-555-RU-001
DATE: 2026-06-12
DEPARTMENT: Fabrication Operations
LOT ID: LOT-2026-02

DRY-ETCH RECIPE QUALIFICATION REPORT: CL_ETCH_M1_0.18

1. NEW RECIPE PARAMETERS (vs Legacy CL_ETCH_M1_0.25)
- Total Etch Time: 144 seconds (120s legacy, +20%)
- Cl2/BCl3 Ratio: 4.5:1 (3.5:1 legacy)
- Chamber Pressure: 8 mTorr (10 mTorr legacy)
- RF Power: 600W (500W legacy)

2. QUALIFICATION WAFER RESULTS
- Etch Rate (0.18um spaces): 385 A/min (baseline: 320 A/min)
- Etch Uniformity: <5% across 200mm wafer
- No micro-loading observed at 0.18um spacing
- Selectivity to photoresist: 8:1

3. VERIFICATION
Three monitor wafers processed. SEM cross-section confirms complete metal clearance with no residual bridging. Recipe approved for production use on Dry-Etch-04 chamber.
